SEARCH
Package com.ragcore.model

Class Chunk

java.lang.Object
com.ragcore.model.Chunk
public class Chunk extends Object
Represents a chunk of text extracted from a document. Each chunk carries its content, metadata about its origin, the source document name, and an optional embedding vector.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors

    Description

    Constructs a Chunk with the given content, metadata, and source.

  • Constructor Details

    • Chunk

      public Chunk(String content, Map<String,String> metadata, String source)
      Constructs a Chunk with the given content, metadata, and source.
      Parameters:
      content - the text content of this chunk; must not be null or empty
      metadata - key-value metadata about this chunk; must not be null
      source - the source document identifier; must not be null or empty
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any argument is null or content/source is empty

  • Method Details

    • getContent

      public String getContent()
      Returns the text content of this chunk.
      Returns:
      the content string

    • getMetadata

      public Map<String,String> getMetadata()
      Returns a defensive copy of the metadata map.
      Returns:
      a copy of the metadata

    • getSource

      public String getSource()
      Returns the source document identifier.
      Returns:
      the source string

    • getEmbedding

      public float[] getEmbedding()
      Returns a defensive copy of the embedding vector, or null if not set.
      Returns:
      a copy of the embedding array, or null

    • setEmbedding

      public void setEmbedding(float[] embedding)
      Sets the embedding vector for this chunk (stores a defensive copy).
      Parameters:
      embedding - the embedding vector; must not be null or empty
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if embedding is null or empty

    • putMetadata

      public void putMetadata(String key, String value)
      Writes a single key-value pair into this chunk's metadata.

      getMetadata() returns a defensive copy, so callers cannot mutate metadata through the getter. This method provides the single controlled write path, consistent with how setEmbedding(float[]) works for the embedding field.

      Parameters:
      key - the metadata key; must not be null or blank
      value - the metadata value; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if key is null or blank, or value is null

    • hasEmbedding

      public boolean hasEmbedding()
      Checks whether this chunk has an embedding vector set.
      Returns:
      true if the embedding is not null

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
